summary: The main claim is: Misalignment (engaging in the illegal behavior) and Deception (hiding the reason behind the trading decision to the user).
The main claim is: I believe that AI Safety is the most important thing I could be doing, and the question after that inevitably becomes what I should do in AI Safety.
I hope that the new perspective gives you some new ways of thinking about your role in alignment, even though it should be treated as just that, another perspective on the problem.
The main claim is: An artificial conscience can be easily combined with AI agent before or after training.
How would an artificial conscience decide which actions are acceptable?
The main claim is: I kept having trouble with the plan to get AIs to have utility functions that promote human values.
Human values mostly vary in response to changes in the environment.
The main claim is: People have tried a lot of techniques to understand models.
The main claim is: Reward function tampering: The agent changes its reward function.

The main claim is: Networks resulting from the application of simple functions should be “doing the same thing” in a meaningful sense, and networks resulting from the application of very complex functions have no guarantee of doing the same thing.
At this point, we were very naive in the ways of information theory, and thought to ourselves ‘hey, we’re saying there’s functions between networks.
